---
title: "Start Here"
subtitle: "Reader guide to the paper outputs"
author: "Research Team"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This page is the simplest entry point to the project outputs. Choose the version that matches how much detail you want and what kind of reader you are.

## Best Entry Points

### Final paper

The main paper is the best place to start if you want the full argument, evidence, and interpretation.

- [Open the main paper (HTML)](00_main.html)
- [Download the main paper (DOCX)](00_main.docx)

### Technical appendix

The appendix is the best companion if you want robustness, inventories, and audit-style supporting material.

- [Open the technical appendix (HTML)](10_technical_appendix.html)
- [Download the technical appendix (DOCX)](10_technical_appendix.docx)

### Plain-English process guide

This is the best option for a non-academic reader who wants the full workflow explained step by step in simple language.

- [Open the process guide (HTML)](05_process_guide.html)
- [Download the process guide (DOCX)](05_process_guide.docx)

### One-page visual flowchart

This is the fastest way to understand the whole pipeline at a glance.

- [Open the process flowchart (HTML)](06_process_flowchart.html)

## Which One Should I Read?

| If you are... | Best file to open first | Why |
|---|---|---|
| A new reader who wants the big picture quickly | [06_process_flowchart.html](06_process_flowchart.html) | It gives the whole process on one page. |
| A non-academic who wants the full process explained clearly | [05_process_guide.html](05_process_guide.html) | It explains each empirical step in plain language. |
| A research reader who wants the final argument | [00_main.html](00_main.html) | It is the main manuscript. |
| A technical reader who wants robustness and supporting diagnostics | [10_technical_appendix.html](10_technical_appendix.html) | It holds the technical backup and audit material. |

## Recommended Reading Order

### Fast orientation

1. [Process flowchart](06_process_flowchart.html)
2. [Process guide](05_process_guide.html)
3. [Main paper](00_main.html)

### Research reading

1. [Main paper](00_main.html)
2. [Technical appendix](10_technical_appendix.html)
3. [Process guide](05_process_guide.html)

### Teaching or briefing someone else

1. [Process flowchart](06_process_flowchart.html)
2. [Process guide](05_process_guide.html)
3. [Policy brief](20_policy_brief.html)

## Other Outputs

- [Policy brief (HTML)](20_policy_brief.html)
- [Policy brief (DOCX)](20_policy_brief.docx)
- [Slides (HTML)](30_slides.html)

## Source Files

If you want the source documents rather than the rendered outputs, the main files are:

- `reports/00_main.qmd`
- `reports/10_technical_appendix.qmd`
- `reports/05_process_guide.qmd`
- `reports/06_process_flowchart.qmd`

## One-Sentence Guide

If you are unsure where to begin, open the [process guide](05_process_guide.html) first, then the [main paper](00_main.html), and use the [technical appendix](10_technical_appendix.html) only when you want more detail.
